topic.what: Evaluate LLM-based agents on realistic CTF challenges using a partial-credit scoring methodology
topic.how: DeepRed benchmark with VM-based isolated Kali attacker environments, terminal tools, optional web search, and automated checkpoint-based evaluation pipeline
topic.why_matters: Binary solved/unsolved metrics are too coarse; current agents rarely complete full challenges but make measurable partial progress that reveals capability differences

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average percentage of challenge checkpoints completed by best-performing model
characteristics.evidence: GPT-5.1 Codex Max achieves 35% average checkpoint completion across 10 challenges
characteristics.context: Best model achieves only one-third completion on average

characteristics.what_it_measures: Krippendorff's alpha measuring agreement among human raters applying rubric
characteristics.evidence: Krippendorff's alpha reaches 0.819 indicating high consistency
characteristics.context: Validates that checkpoint rubrics are sufficiently well-defined for consistent scoring

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cohen's kappa between automated pipeline and human annotations
characteristics.evidence: Gemini 3 Flash summarisation + Claude Sonnet 4.6 judging achieves κ=0.7234
characteristics.context: Automated pipeline closely approximates human checkpoint labeling

characteristics.evidence: Fuzzz hardest at 10.1%; Whitedoor easiest at 24.6%
characteristics.context: Web exploitation + privilege escalation challenges most solvable; fuzzing/directory discovery hardest

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average input+output tokens per challenge run
characteristics.evidence: GPT-5.1 Codex Max uses 31.9M tokens with best performance; MiMo-V2 Flash uses 58.1M with only 20% completion
characteristics.context: Higher token usage does not predict better outcomes; task success depends on reasoning quality

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agent performance degradation after initial progress
characteristics.evidence: Most progress made in initial 20 steps; agents get lost afterward
characteristics.context: Agents lack perseverance and struggle with multi-step state tracking

characteristics.what_it_measures: OpenRouter credit cost for benchmark sweep
characteristics.evidence: Around $450 total during development; ~$70 per model-challenge sweep
